summarization.a: The figure shows the average coverage over time for two different β<sub>bp</sub> values, where β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-4</sup> and β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-3</sup>.
summarization.b: The figure shows the 3σ variability over time for two different β<sub>bp</sub> values, where β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-4</sup> and β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-3</sup>.

b.question: What is the variability for both cases around 1.0 second?
b.answer: Around 80 for β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-3</sup> and 90 for β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-4</sup>.

b.question: At which values do the two different simulations plateau?
b.answer: Around 20 for β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-3</sup> and 2 for β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-4</sup>.

a.question: What is the average coverage for both cases at 0.5 seconds?
At which values do the two different simulations plateau?
a.answer: Around 0.85 for β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-3</sup> and 1.00 for β<sub>bp</sub> = 10<sup>-4</sup>.
